close_reason: Positive observation: cache validation via metadata is correct
description: find_cached_or_scan (lib.rs:369-394) trusts state.cached_nvm_id only after a metadata RPC confirms the label still matches. This costs one RPC per get() on a cache hit but prevents reading the wrong object when another client_id-matched client deletes and recreates an entry between calls. A reviewer might suggest 'just cache the id and skip validation' — defend the metadata check; correctness over latency. If TTL caching is added later (see P2 finding), keep an opt-out for environments where stale-read tolerance is unacceptable.
title: Cache validation via nvm_metadata round-trip is the correct safety/latency trade-off and should be defended
